speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: Go, Captain, from me greet the Danish king.

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: Tell him that by his license Fortinbras

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: Craves the conveyance of a promised march

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: Over his kingdom. You know the rendezvous.

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: If that his Majesty would aught with us,

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: We shall express our duty in his eye;

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: And let him know so.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: I will do ’t, my lord.

speaker: FORTINBRAS
text: Go softly on.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Good sir, whose powers are these?

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: They are of Norway, sir.

speaker: HAMLET
text: How purposed, sir, I pray you?

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Against some part of Poland.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Who commands them, sir?

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: The nephew to old Norway, Fortinbras.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Goes it against the main of Poland, sir,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Or for some frontier?

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Truly to speak, and with no addition,

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: We go to gain a little patch of ground

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: That hath in it no profit but the name.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: To pay five ducats, five, I would not farm it;

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Nor will it yield to Norway or the Pole

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: A ranker rate, should it be sold in fee.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why, then, the Polack never will defend it.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: Yes, it is already garrisoned.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Two thousand souls and twenty thousand ducats

speaker: HAMLET
text: Will not debate the question of this straw.

speaker: HAMLET
text: This is th’ impostume of much wealth and peace,

speaker: HAMLET
text: That inward breaks and shows no cause without

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why the man dies.—I humbly thank you, sir.

speaker: CAPTAIN
text: God be wi’ you, sir.

speaker: ROSENCRANTZ
text: Will ’t please you go, my lord?

speaker: HAMLET
text: I’ll be with you straight. Go a little before.

speaker: HAMLET
text: How all occasions do inform against me

speaker: HAMLET
text: And spur my dull revenge. What is a man

speaker: HAMLET
text: If his chief good and market of his time

speaker: HAMLET
text: Be but to sleep and feed? A beast, no more.

speaker: HAMLET
text: Sure He that made us with such large discourse,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Looking before and after, gave us not

speaker: HAMLET
text: That capability and godlike reason

speaker: HAMLET
text: To fust in us unused. Now whether it be

speaker: HAMLET
text: Bestial oblivion or some craven scruple

speaker: HAMLET
text: Of thinking too precisely on th’ event

speaker: HAMLET
text: (A thought which, quartered, hath but one part wisdom

speaker: HAMLET
text: And ever three parts coward), I do not know

speaker: HAMLET
text: Why yet I live to say “This thing’s to do,”

speaker: HAMLET
text: Sith I have cause, and will, and strength, and means

speaker: HAMLET
text: To do ’t. Examples gross as Earth exhort me:

speaker: HAMLET
text: Witness this army of such mass and charge,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Led by a delicate and tender prince,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Whose spirit with divine ambition puffed

speaker: HAMLET
text: Makes mouths at the invisible event,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Exposing what is mortal and unsure

speaker: HAMLET
text: To all that fortune, death, and danger dare,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Even for an eggshell. Rightly to be great

speaker: HAMLET
text: Is not to stir without great argument,

speaker: HAMLET
text: But greatly to find quarrel in a straw

speaker: HAMLET
text: When honor’s at the stake. How stand I, then,

speaker: HAMLET
text: That have a father killed, a mother stained,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Excitements of my reason and my blood,

speaker: HAMLET
text: And let all sleep, while to my shame I see

speaker: HAMLET
text: The imminent death of twenty thousand men

speaker: HAMLET
text: That for a fantasy and trick of fame

speaker: HAMLET
text: Go to their graves like beds, fight for a plot

speaker: HAMLET
text: Whereon the numbers cannot try the cause,

speaker: HAMLET
text: Which is not tomb enough and continent

speaker: HAMLET
text: To hide the slain? O, from this time forth

speaker: HAMLET
text: My thoughts be bloody or be nothing worth!
